seoTitle: Hazelwood South vs Heath Hill — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hazelwood South and Heath Hill. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Hazelwood South vs Heath Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hazelwood South and Heath Hill.
heading: Hazelwood South vs Heath Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hazelwood South, Victoria and Heath Hill, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hazelwood South has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Heath Hill (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hazelwood South or Heath Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hazelwood South has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Heath Hill scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hazelwood South scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Heath Hill scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hazelwood South or Heath Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hazelwood South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
